title: Inconsistencies and registry-quality issues

items.title: Three schema layers have zero records
items.observation: architecture_graph_report.json reports node_count=0 for api-product, legal-evidence, and observability-operability. legal-evidence is the core PRD concept (Evidence-first principle); api-product covers Legal Nexus API surface; observability-operability covers latency/throughput/audit metrics.
items.implication: Verifier success does not imply coverage of legal-evidence, api-product, or observability-operability. Downstream agents could read green verifier output as full architectural coverage.
items.verification: Add at least one anchored item record per empty layer (requirement, proof_gate, or evidence) and rerun S02/S03/S04.
non_claims: Does not assert that empty layers indicate missing product capability.
Does not promote any specific record into the registry.

items.title: 65% of nodes are high or critical risk
items.observation: 15 of 23 nodes (11 high, 4 critical) carry high or critical risk_level. Distribution is concentrated in governance and security-safety layers (M001-ARCHITECTURE-ONLY-GUARDRAIL, REQ-R022, REQ-R028, RISK-OVERCLAIM-RUNTIME).
items.implication: High-risk concentration in non-product layers signals that the registry currently documents its own governance, not product architecture. Product layers contain only 6 of 23 nodes.
items.verification: After adding product-layer records, recompute high/critical share and confirm risk distribution reflects actual product surface.
non_claims: Does not assert that high-risk classification is incorrect.
Does not assert current product runtime failure.

items.title: Three orphan nodes with no edges
items.observation: architecture_graph_report.json lists ASSUMP-PRD-SOURCE-TRUTH, S05-OLD-PROJECT-PRIOR-ART, and S10-GIGAEMBEDDINGS-CHALLENGER-BLOCKED as isolated-node orphans. ASSUMP-PRD-SOURCE-TRUTH is a foundational assumption that should be linked to DEC-D031 via has_assumption.
items.implication: Schema enforces edge endpoint resolution but does not enforce minimum connectivity. Orphan critical records weaken traceability and decision-fitness coverage.
items.verification: Add edges: EDGE-DEC-D031-HAS-ASSUMPTION-PRD-SOURCE-TRUTH (has_assumption), and equivalent traceability edges for the other two orphans.
non_claims: Does not assert that orphan nodes should be deleted.
Does not assert that connectivity proves correctness.

items.title: Six weakly connected components in a 23-node graph
items.observation: Graph contains components of size 14, 4, 2, 1, 1, 1. A mature registry typically converges on 1–2 components with explicit justification.
items.implication: Fragmented graph reduces the usefulness of graph-distance, centrality, and reachability analyses planned by downstream slices.
items.verification: Reduce component count to ≤2 by adding evidence/traceability edges across the size-4 and size-2 clusters.
non_claims: Does not assert a target component count is the only acceptable shape.
Does not assert that connectivity equals product readiness.

items.title: generated-cypher layer has only one record
items.observation: Layer generated-cypher contains only REQ-R017. This layer corresponds to one of the highest-risk capability surfaces (LLM-generated Cypher safety, injection risk, KnowQL boundary). It should host proof gates, risks, interfaces, and workflow checks.
items.implication: Critical-risk surface is under-modeled. Verifier cannot enforce overclaim or proof gates that do not exist as records.
items.verification: Add at least proof_gate for generated-Cypher safety, risk for injection, and a workflow_check for Cypher linter.
non_claims: Does not assert that generated-Cypher behavior is currently unsafe.
Does not specify implementation of a Cypher linter.

items.title: Decision fitness gap: high-risk decision lacks checked_by/validated_by coverage
items.observation: README states that high/critical active decisions must carry checked_by or validated_by edges to a proof gate or workflow check. DEC-D031 (risk_level=high) has EDGE-DEC-D031-CHECKED-BY-CHECK-ARCHITECTURE-EXTRACTOR present, but DEC-D032 (risk_level=medium) has none. If DEC-D032 is later upgraded to high, the rule would trip.
items.implication: Latent fitness failure if risk_level is reclassified upward without simultaneous edge addition.
items.verification: Either add an explicit checked_by edge for DEC-D032 referencing CHECK-ARCHITECTURE-EXTRACTOR or S05 skill consistency check, or document why medium-risk decisions are exempt.
non_claims: Does not assert DEC-D032 must be upgraded.
Does not assert current verifier failure.

items.title: Duplicated non_claims across multiple requirement records
items.observation: REQ-R001, REQ-R009, and REQ-R010 each carry an identical seven-entry non_claims list (No legal-answer correctness claim, No product Legal KnowQL behavior claim, No parser completeness claim, No product retrieval quality claim, No managed embedding API fallback claim, No production-scale FalkorDB claim, No LLM legal authority claim).
items.implication: Boilerplate non_claims dilute their per-record value and increase maintenance cost when the global baseline shifts.
items.verification: Either move the shared seven into a registry-wide non_claims baseline (with schema support) and keep only record-specific deltas, or document why per-record duplication is intentional.
non_claims: Does not assert that current non_claims are incorrect.
Does not propose a specific schema change.

items.title: Source anchors to selector-only without line ranges
items.observation: GATE-G005, GATE-G008, GATE-G011, GATE-G015 anchor to prd/04_review_findings.md via selector only (G-005, G-008, G-011, G-015) without line_start/line_end or quote_hash. README S04 verifier states it checks that selector text still appears in the anchored file, but reformatting can change selector visibility.
items.implication: Anchor fragility under upstream reformatting; selector-only anchors are convenient but fail silently if the selector text is rewritten.
items.verification: Add line_start/line_end ranges, or quote_hash, to anchors that currently rely on selectors only.
non_claims: Does not assert current selectors are broken.
Does not require deletion of selector field.

items.title: Confidence semantics on edges versus item proof_level
items.observation: Edge schema defines confidence (0..1) but item schema does not. Combination semantics are not documented: if an edge with confidence 0.75 connects two high-risk active items with proof_level=source-anchor, the composite trust score is unspecified.
items.implication: Risk that downstream consumers interpret confidence inconsistently (probability vs evidence strength vs reviewer subjective belief).
items.verification: Document the meaning of edge.confidence in README and either remove or align it with proof_level semantics for items.
non_claims: Does not assert that current confidence values are incorrect.
Does not propose a specific composition formula.

items.title: Health dashboard and architecture report duplicate content
items.observation: architecture_health.md and architecture_report.md present the same Layer Coverage, Open Proof Gates, High-Risk Nodes, and Orphan Findings tables. They differ mainly in emoji markers and headings.
items.implication: Two derived files drift independently and double the maintenance surface for the views generator.
items.verification: Either consolidate into one human-facing view, or redefine each: report = S03 technical snapshot, dashboard = planning-oriented quick stats.
non_claims: Does not assert that both views should be deleted.
Does not specify which file is canonical.

items.title: Ten edge types defined but never used
items.observation: Edge type enum contains 22 values; architecture_edges.jsonl uses only checked_by, satisfies, depends_on, blocks, bounded_by, evidenced_by (6 types). Unused: implements, refines, contradicts, supersedes, superseded_by, risks, owned_by, uses_viewpoint, has_option, chosen_over, has_consequence, has_assumption, has_constraint, implicates, reviewed_by, governs_artifact.
items.implication: Schema permits more semantic richness than the current registry uses. Some unused edges (has_assumption, has_consequence) would directly resolve R04-INC-003 orphan findings.
items.verification: Either populate the missing edge types where applicable (especially has_assumption for ASSUMP-PRD-SOURCE-TRUTH), or document why they remain reserved for later milestones.
non_claims: Does not assert that all enum values must be used now.
Does not propose schema reduction.

items.title: Schema-version locked at v1 with no migration policy
items.observation: architecture.schema.json defines schema_version as const 'legalgraph-architecture-registry/v1'. No documented strategy for evolving to v2 (new enum values, new required fields, deprecation handling).
items.implication: Any backward-incompatible change to enums or required fields requires either silent rollover or a coordinated migration that is currently undefined.
items.verification: Add a 'Schema evolution' section to README describing the v1→v2 path: parallel acceptance window, extractor migration, verifier behavior on mixed schema_version, and deprecation log.
non_claims: Does not propose a specific v2 schema.
Does not require immediate version change.

items.title: Claims ledger mixes registry-mechanics claims with product claims
items.observation: CHECK-ARCHITECTURE-EXTRACTOR is classified as safe-to-say in claims_ledger.md alongside requirement records. It is a workflow_check about the extractor, not a product claim. The ledger does not distinguish 'safe to say about the registry' from 'safe to say about the product'.
items.implication: Future agents may quote registry-mechanics claims as if they were product-readiness claims.
items.verification: Add a 'claim domain' column (registry vs product) to claims_ledger.md, or split the ledger into two derived views.
non_claims: Does not assert that the current classification is wrong.
Does not require ledger schema redesign.

items.title: Three derived planning views share generator and freshness gate
items.observation: architecture_health.md, product_readiness_blockers.md, and claims_ledger.md are all generated by scripts/generate-architecture-views.py with a single --check gate. A partial regeneration is not supported.
items.implication: Any edit to one view triggers regeneration semantics for all three; isolated regeneration for testing is not part of the contract.
items.verification: Document whether per-view --check flags are supported, or accept that the bundle is atomic by design.
non_claims: Does not assert per-view isolation is required.
Does not propose splitting the generator.

items.title: Layer enum lacks a finance/cost-control layer
items.observation: Schema layer enum covers legal-evidence, temporal-model, graph-runtime, parser-ingestion, retrieval-embedding, generated-cypher, api-product, security-safety, observability-operability, workflow-governance, architecture-governance. There is no layer for cost/quota concerns (embedding-API spend, FalkorDB resource limits, LLM token budget).
items.implication: Cost/quota proof gates currently have no canonical layer; they would default to observability-operability or security-safety, blurring those layers.
items.verification: Either extend the layer enum (requires v2 schema, see R04-INC-012) or document the chosen home for cost/quota records.
non_claims: Does not assert that cost layer must be added.
Does not propose specific cost gates.
Inconsistencies are observations of the registry's current shape, not failures of the underlying product.
Does not assert that verifier currently fails on any item above.
Does not promote any item into architecture_items.jsonl.
